title: CLAUDE.md
text: DSIR small R package (“Data Science Infrastructure Global Health R”), targeted CRAN. provides: ggplot2 theme (theme_dsi) flextable defaults helper (dsi_flextable_defaults) pie-chart wrapper (ggpie) Bundled Member State data: who_countries tibble plus regional ISO3 vectors (wpro_cty, afro_cty, amro_cty, searo_cty, euro_cty, emro_cty, pic_cty) lookup helpers (iso3_to_region(), iso3_to_m49()) geomean() ratio-based indicator aggregation Thin clients Global Health Observatory OData API (gho_indicators, gho_data, gho_dimensions, gho_clean, plus availability helpers gho_has_data, gho_count, gho_coverage) Thin clients UN SDG API (sdg_goals, sdg_targets, sdg_indicators, sdg_areas, sdg_data, sdg_clean, plus series-exploration helper sdg_coverage) package CRAN-bound, conventions driven R CMD check CRAN policy, just taste.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Breaking: unified 15-column cleaned-indicator schema. gho_clean() sdg_clean() now produce shape: GHO SDG output can bind_rows()-ed (formally, bind_indicators()-ed) without column-shape work. source "gho" "sdg". value stays raw character (preserves SDG "<0.1" similar); value_num numeric coercion (NA coercion fails). year integer. iso3 GHO set SpatialDim %% who_countries$iso3 else NA; SDG comes m49_to_iso3(geoAreaCode). SDG specifically: id now holds indicator code (e.g. "3.4.1", formerly named indicator) indicator holds seriesDescription text API. pre-0.7.0 SDG columns goal target longer carried forward. series SDG series code (e.g. "SH_DTH_NCD"). dim1 / dim2 / dim3 always NA SDG (GHO-). GHO specifically: series always NA (SDG-concept). location_name resolved location who_countries$name_short plus hardcoded regional/GLOBAL map (see location_name fix ). dim1 / dim2 / dim3 come Dim1 / Dim2 / Dim3. New functions: - bind_indicators(...) — variadic, rbinds number cleaned tibbles one. Validates input full 15-column schema; drops NULL arguments; column re-ordering across inputs tolerated. - m49_to_iso3(m49) — counterpart iso3_to_m49(). Accepts zero-padded ("076") bare ("76") input. Non-Member areas region/world aggregates return NA. Used internally sdg_clean() populate iso3. Bug fixes: - sdg_coverage() duplicate definition. function defined R/sdg.R R/sdg_coverage.R 0.6.0; R loads files alphabetically sdg_coverage.R version overrode one sdg.R. two implementations subtly different behaviour. Resolved deleting version sdg.R hardening survivor (separator \r → \x1f, added upfront .resolve_area(area) “dropped unknown ISO3” warning surfaces sdg_coverage() rather swallowed suppressWarnings(sdg_data(...))). - .gho_get() retry parity .sdg_get() — added req_timeout(20) backoff = ~ min(2 ^ .x, 30). change applied inline HTTP call gho_count() (doesn’t go .gho_get needs @odata.count rather value). - sdg_data() multi-page rbind failure. 0.6.0 fix relied make.row.names = TRUE (default) disambiguate per-page tibble row names like "1".."1000". actually disambiguate page 1000 rows numbered identically; rbind still trips duplicate 'row.names' allowed. Surfaced R CMD check --run-donttest ran sdg_data("1.1.1") (26+ pages ~1000 rows). Fixed passing make.row.names = FALSE explicitly. existing test weak catch (mock page 1 row); new regression test uses 2-row pages collision actually fires. - sdg_data() example simplified. first example sdg_data("1.1.1") (unfiltered, 26+ pages) — slow R CMD check. Now sdg_data("1.1.1", area = "PHL"). - gho_clean() / sdg_clean() cross-API location_name consistency. fix, gho_clean() left location_name always NA (GHO data endpoint returns name field), sdg_clean() used SDG API’s raw geoAreaName. two sides disagree spelling country, breaking bind_indicators() consumers group location_name. New helpers .gho_resolve_location_name() .sdg_resolve_location_name() (R/gho.R / R/sdg.R) route cleaners who_countries$name_short Member States. GHO additionally resolves regional codes AFR / AMR / SEAR / EUR / EMR / WPR / GLOBAL human names via hardcoded map. SDG falls back raw geoAreaName non-Member rows (region/world aggregates), preserving information who_countries carry. Schema, column order, types unchanged — zero breaking change. - Network helpers now fail soft malformed response bodies, just HTTP errors. .gho_get(), .sdg_get(), inline HTTP call gho_count() wrap httr2::resp_body_json() tryCatch(). truncated upstream body (premature EOF) now surfaces cli_warn() returns NULL / NA_integer_ instead propagating jsonlite parse error — otherwise abort R CMD check example run (CRAN-blocking). See NEWS.md user-facing entry. - cli::cli_warn() glue-injection bug warning handlers (fixed 2026-05-21). six tryCatch error handlers R/gho.R / R/sdg.R passed conditionMessage(e) directly message element: cli::cli_warn(c(..., "x" = conditionMessage(e))). cli runs glue interpolation every message string, error message containing literal { / } makes cli try evaluate glue expression re-error reporting original error. HTTP error messages never carry braces, latent — jsonlite parse error message embeds offending JSON fragment, always braces, new body-parse handlers tripped first malformed-body test. Fixed assigning msg <- conditionMessage(e) interpolating variable: "x" = "{msg}" (glue inserts variable’s value verbatim recurse ). Applied six handlers — three new body-parse ones three pre-existing HTTP-failure ones — pattern uniform. Documentation: - network examples switched \dontrun{} \donttest{}. CRAN reviewers can opt run ; R CMD check --run-donttest exercises locally. - DESCRIPTION Authors@R now carries user’s ORCID (0000-0002-8831-1684). - cran-comments.md rewritten 0.7.0 resubmission note (explains 0.2.0 → 0.7.0 jump, points NEWS unified-schema breaking change, confirms --run-donttest also clean). pre-0.7.0 file still initial-submission template. Internal: - New R/clean_schema.R centralises 15-column schema (.dsi_clean_schema()), typed-empty-tibble template (.dsi_empty_clean()), typed-NA filler (.fill_na(n, type)). change schema start . - httptest2 added Suggests. mocking tests use httr2::with_mocked_responses() directly (httptest2 peer httr2 offline testing); see implementation notes gotcha. - test-gho-dimensions.R added — gho_dimensions() last exported network function without offline coverage. Uses mock_json() pattern test-gho-get-mock.R / test-sdg-get-mock.R. - Three regression tests malformed-body fail-soft path (added 2026-05-21). test-gho-get-mock.R gained two (.gho_get() truncated JSON body → warning + NULL; gho_count() truncated body → NA_integer_) test-sdg-get-mock.R gained one (.sdg_get() truncated body → warning + NULL). gho_count() previously offline coverage . Writing .sdg_get one surfaced cli_warn() glue-injection bug listed Bug fixes — malformed-body fix shipped NEWS.md without test, bug fix went unnoticed test written.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: unified schema’s source truth R/clean_schema.R. .dsi_clean_schema() returns canonical column order type code ("chr", "int", "num"). .dsi_empty_clean() produces typed 0-row tibble used cleaners empty input bind_indicators() -input / -NULL case. Adding new column means updating file gho_clean() / sdg_clean() populate . gho_clean()’s old n = 0 bug: pre-0.7.0 cleaners used rep(NA, n), n = 0 produces logical(0). empty-output column types therefore logical, matching documented types. 0.7.0 fixes routing empty input .dsi_empty_clean() directly. M49 column who_countries$m49_code, un_m49. Values 3-char zero-padded (e.g. "076" Brazil). SDG API accepts "076" "76"; m49_to_iso3() normalises lookup via formatC(.integer(m49), width = 3, flag = "0") — note padded[padded == "NA"] <- NA_character_ step handles formatC(NA_integer_) returning literal string "NA". GHO iso3 heuristic. gho_clean() sets iso3 = location location %% who_countries$iso3, else NA. pure-lookup check, regex — weird--valid 3-letter codes like "COG" work, region codes ("EUR", "GLOBAL") correctly get NA. location_name resolution. cleaners delegate small helper next public function: .gho_resolve_location_name(location) R/gho.R .sdg_resolve_location_name(iso3, raw_geo_area_name) R/sdg.R. GHO order resolution: ISO3 → who_countries$name_short, else regional code → hardcoded region_names map (AFR/AMR/SEAR/EUR/EMR/WPR/GLOBAL), else NA. SDG order: ISO3 → who_countries$name_short, else SDG API raw geoAreaName, else NA. hardcoded regional map intentionally GHO-— SDG geoAreaName already carries readable names M49 region/world aggregates ("World", "Sub-Saharan Africa", etc.), duplicating map SDG side redundant fragile. “fallback geoAreaName” rule means SDG output retains location_name rows ISO3 NA (region/world aggregates), GHO output NA unknown/non-Member spatial codes. asymmetry design: GHO equivalent geoAreaName fall back . SDG indicator column source. Comes seriesDescription raw sdg_data() response, indicator-list endpoint. series, seriesDescription absent; case indicator NA. want human label , join result sdg_indicators() id. .resolve_area() (R/sdg.R) detects format ^[-Za-z]{3}$ ISO3 ^[0-9]+$ M49. Mixed input errors. Unknown ISO3 codes dropped warning; every code unknown, function errors. sdg_coverage() calls .resolve_area() upfront legitimate “dropped unknown ISO3” warning surfaces sdg_coverage() rather swallowed suppressWarnings(sdg_data(...)) wrapper. sdg_data() re-runs .resolve_area() already-resolved vector — ’s -op (M49 passes ), duplication safe. grouping key paste(location, series, sep = "\x1f"); \x1f (US, unit separator) appear either M49 numeric code SDG series code. httr2 1.x mocking gotcha. httr2::with_mocked_responses(mock, code) requires mock function, list, NULL — passing bare httr2::response() object errors mock must function, list, NULL. mock_json() helper test files wraps single response list(...); chaining two pages, tests use c(mock_json(p1), mock_json(p2)) concatenate two length-1 lists length-2 list. URL-capturing tests pass function(req) mock can inspect outgoing URL returning canned response. gho_count() uses HTTP call site, .gho_get(), needs @odata.count response envelope, value. touch retry/timeout config one, mirror change (currently sync). 20-second timeout .sdg_get() / .gho_get() fine typical 2–6s responses, un.org endpoint occasionally spikes well past 20s. users start reporting spurious “Timeout reached” failures, bumping constant 30–60s right move. req_retry() runs three attempts, worst-case wall time bounded around 66s × 1 retry cycle ≈ several minutes. devtools::document() may need two passes new function adds roxygen cross-reference (@seealso [new_fn()]). First pass writes new Rd; second pass resolves cross-reference. cli::cli_warn() untrusted error text. Never pass conditionMessage(e) — string may contain { / } — bare element cli_warn() message vector; cli glue-interpolates every element. Assign variable first interpolate variable: msg <- conditionMessage(e); cli::cli_warn(c(..., "x" = "{msg}")). glue inserts variable’s value literally re-interpolate . six tryCatch warning handlers R/gho.R / R/sdg.R follow pattern — keep new handler consistent.

title: DSIR
text: who_countries tibble lists 194 Member States ISO3, ISO2, UN M49 codes, official names, short names, region. Western Pacific countries, extra column is_pic identifies 14 Pacific Island Countries. convenience, DSIR offers pre-defined vectors ISO3 codes region. is_pic flag useful Pacific Island Countries often analysed group, given distinct demographic geographic profiles. vector ISO3 codes need know region belongs , iso3_to_region() provides lookup. vectorised returns NA codes match Member State. convenient joining external datasets (often arrive keyed ISO3) regional structure. companion helper iso3_to_m49() converts ISO3 codes UN M49 numeric codes — useful GHO API keyed ISO3 ("PHL") UN SDG API keyed M49 ("608"). M49 values returned three-character zero-padded strings, exactly stored who_countries$m49_code. practice can usually skip explicit conversion: sdg_data() sdg_coverage() accept ISO3 codes area argument lookup internally (see SDG section ).

title: DSIR
text: gho_clean() produces unified DSIR cleaned-indicator schema — 15-column shape sdg_clean(). Columns include source ("gho"), id, indicator, location, iso3, location_name (empty GHO), year, value, value_num, low, high, series (empty GHO), three optional GHO dimensions dim1–dim3. Columns absent raw response filled typed NA.

title: DSIR
text: health indicators constructed geometric mean component values rather arithmetic mean. UHC Service Coverage Index, example, aggregates 14 tracer indicators using nested geometric means. DSIR provides geomean() : geomean() handles missing values, zeros, negative values sensibly — see ?geomean details. small helper, removes common source bugs re-implementing index calculations indicator components.
code: # Unweighted geometric mean geomean(c(0.6, 0.8, 0.95)) #> [1] 0.7697002 #> 0.7720589  # With optional weights — useful when tracers have different  # methodological importance geomean(c(0.6, 0.8, 0.95), w = c(2, 1, 1)) #> [1] 0.7232343

title: DSIR
text: DSIR provides two paired ggplot2 themes tuned -style charts — clean panels, modest grids, consistent accent colour. Use drop-replacements theme_minimal() theme_bw() respectively whenever chart heading deliverable. rule thumb simple: single-panel plots use theme_dsi(), faceted plots use theme_dsi_facet(). two share typography, title treatment, legend handling, differ frame data — facet variant adds panel borders, light strip backgrounds, breathing room panels, look heavy single-panel chart.

title: DSIR
text: theme_dsi() keeps chart chrome minimal — half-frame axis, light grid lines, -blue accent axis line. default grid runs directions; pass grid = "y" minimalist horizontal-look.  bar charts, pair theme_dsi() scale_y_dsi_col() (scale_x_dsi_col() value mapped x) — thin wrappers around scale_*_continuous() remove lower axis expansion, columns sit flush axis instead floating .
code: uhc_clean |>   filter(iso3 %in% c("AUS", "CHN", "PHL", "FJI")) |>   left_join(who_countries, by = "iso3") |>   ggplot(aes(x = year, y = value_num, group = iso3, color = name_short)) +   geom_line(linewidth = .8) +   geom_point(size = 1.8) +   theme_dsi() +   labs(     title    = "UHC Service Coverage Index, selected WPR Member States",     subtitle = "2015 onwards",     x = NULL, y = "SCI", color = NULL   ) uhc_clean |>   filter(year == max(year)) |>   left_join(who_countries, by = "iso3") |>   arrange(desc(value_num)) |>   head(10) |>   ggplot(aes(reorder(name_short, value_num), value_num)) +   geom_col(fill = "#0093D5") +   coord_flip() +   scale_y_dsi_col() +   theme_dsi(grid = "x") +   labs(     title    = "UHC Service Coverage Index, top 10 WPR Member States",     subtitle = "Latest available year",     x = NULL, y = "SCI"   )

title: DSIR
text: chart split across many small panels, half-frame look becomes visually noisy — accent-blue axis line repeats across every facet. theme_dsi_facet() switches full panel border, adds light grey strip background clearly mark facet’s label, introduces panel spacing adjacent panels don’t run together.  strip_fill argument lets change strip background colour emphasis — example, light-blue tone derived accent deliverable strips carry meaning:
code: uhc_clean |>   left_join(who_countries, by = "iso3") |>   filter(is_pic) |>   ggplot(aes(x = year, y = value_num)) +   geom_line(color = "#0093D5", linewidth = 0.8) +   geom_point(color = "#0093D5", size = 1.5) +   facet_wrap(~ name_short, ncol = 4) +   theme_dsi_facet() +   labs(     title    = "UHC Service Coverage Index, Pacific Island Countries",     subtitle = "Each panel shows one country's trajectory",     x = NULL, y = "SCI"   ) uhc_clean |>   left_join(who_countries, by = "iso3") |>   filter(is_pic) |>   ggplot(aes(x = year, y = value_num)) +   geom_line(color = "#0093D5", linewidth = 0.8) +   facet_wrap(~ name_short, ncol = 4) +   theme_dsi_facet(strip_fill = "#E5F4FB") +   labs(title = "UHC SCI, PIC — with custom strip colour",        x = NULL, y = "SCI")

title: DSIR
text: dsi_flextable_defaults() sets -style defaults flextable globally — booktabs theme, bold headers, modest padding. Call near top report every subsequent flextable() picks formatting. UHC SCI WPR, latest year name_short value_num Australia 89 New Zealand 89 Republic Korea 88 Singapore 88 Japan 86 China 85 Brunei Darussalam 84 Malaysia 80 Cook Islands 75 Palau 75 Tonga 71 Viet Nam 71 Mongolia 70 Fiji 69 Philippines 69 Indonesia 67 Niue 67 Marshall Islands 66 Micronesia 65 Tuvalu 65 Lao PDR 64 Cambodia 62 Nauru 62 Samoa 62 Vanuatu 52 Kiribati 51 Solomon Islands 47 Papua New Guinea 32
code: library(flextable) dsi_flextable_defaults(font_family = "Geogria")  uhc_clean |>   filter(year == max(year)) |>   left_join(who_countries, by = "iso3") |>   select(name_short, value_num) |>   arrange(desc(value_num)) |>   flextable() |>   set_table_properties("autofit", width = .6) %>%   set_caption("UHC SCI in WPR, latest year")

title: DSIR
text: sdg_data() sdg_clean() follow fetch--tidy pattern GHO counterparts. main differences indicator codes use dotted SDG format (e.g. "3.4.1") value, low, high kept character — SDG API returns non-numeric entries ("<0.1", aggregate notes) rows, coerce .numeric() ready drop . sdg_indicators() accepts optional search argument behaviour gho_indicators() — multiple keywords -ed together matched case-insensitively indicator description. filter runs client-side UN SDG indicator list short (~250 rows) endpoint OData. area argument sdg_data() sdg_coverage() accepts either ISO3 codes (converted internally via iso3_to_m49()) UN M49 numeric codes — DSIR’s regional vectors (wpro_cty, afro_cty, etc.) work directly, way GHO client. mix two formats single call. sdg_clean() produces 15-column schema gho_clean(), two outputs can combined directly bind_indicators(). SDG rows populate series column (iso3 column via [m49_to_iso3()] Member States), leaving GHO-dim1–dim3 columns NA.

what: Combining GHO and SDG with bind_indicators()
title: DSIR

title: DSIR
text: single SDG indicator often contains several series — example different vaccines, sex strata, causes death — country year coverage. Indicator "3.b.1" (vaccine coverage) clear case: published four separate series (DTP3, MCV2, PCV3, HPV), year coverage newer vaccines much shorter DTP3. sdg_coverage() summarises year range observation count per (location, series), can inspect series exist covered deciding one analyse. Note DSIR intentionally provide SDG analogues gho_has_data() gho_count(). SDG data generally complete enough screening helpers add little value — useful pre-analysis question SDG “series available?”, sdg_coverage() answers.

title: DSIR — Data Science Infrastructure for Global Health
text: who_countries — tibble 194 Member States ISO3, ISO2, UN M49 codes, official short names, region, is_pic flag Pacific Island Countries. Regional ISO3 vectors — convenience character vectors region, derived who_countries: iso3_to_region() — quick lookup ISO3 codes regions. Vectorized; returns NA codes matching Member State. iso3_to_m49() / m49_to_iso3() — convert ISO3 codes UN M49 numeric codes either direction. Useful moving GHO (ISO3) SDG (M49) workflows. Case-insensitive ISO3 side; accept zero-padded bare M49 input M49 side; non-Member areas return NA. practice rarely need call directly: sdg_data() sdg_coverage() accept ISO3 codes area argument convert via iso3_to_m49() internally, sdg_clean() populates iso3 column via m49_to_iso3().
code: library(DSIR) library(dplyr)  who_countries  # Filter Member States in the Western Pacific Region who_countries |>   filter(who_region == "WPR") |>   select(iso3, name_short, is_pic) wpro_cty   # 28 Western Pacific Member States (since May 2025) afro_cty   # 47 African Region Member States amro_cty   # 35 Region of the Americas Member States searo_cty  # 10 South-East Asia Region Member States euro_cty   # 53 European Region Member States emro_cty   # 21 Eastern Mediterranean Region Member States pic_cty    # 14 Pacific Island Country Member States (subset of WPR) iso3_to_region("PHL")                          # "WPR" iso3_to_region(c("PHL", "FRA", "ZAF", "XYZ"))  # "WPR" "EUR" "AFR" NA iso3_to_m49("PHL")                             # "608" iso3_to_m49(c("PHL", "FRA", "JPN"))            # "608" "250" "392" iso3_to_m49(c("PRI", "PHL"))                   # NA "608"  m49_to_iso3(c("608", "250", "392"))            # "PHL" "FRA" "JPN" m49_to_iso3(c("076", "76"))                    # "BRA" "BRA" m49_to_iso3("900")                             # NA  (world aggregate)

title: DSIR — Data Science Infrastructure for Global Health
text: theme_dsi() theme_dsi_facet() — publication-ready ggplot2 themes. Use theme_dsi() single-panel charts theme_dsi_facet() faceted plots; facet variant adds panel borders, light strip backgrounds, panel spacing tuned multi-panel layouts. scale_y_dsi_col() scale_x_dsi_col() — drop-replacements scale_y_continuous() scale_x_continuous() remove default lower expansion, columns bar charts sit flush axis instead floating . Pick one matches mapped value: scale_y_dsi_col() value y aesthetic (including horizontal bars made coord_flip() — aesthetic still y), scale_x_dsi_col() value x aesthetic (e.g.  geom_col(aes(value, category))). accept argument scale_*_continuous() accepts. dsi_flextable_defaults() — one-line setup flextable formatting (booktabs style, bold headers, paddings). ggpie() — quick pie charts automatic percentage labels.
code: library(ggplot2) library(dplyr)  # Single panel — theme_dsi() who_countries |>   count(who_region) |>   ggplot(aes(reorder(who_region, n), n)) +   geom_col(fill = "#0093D5") +   coord_flip() +   scale_y_dsi_col() +   theme_dsi() +   labs(title = "WHO Member States by region", x = NULL, y = NULL)  # Faceted — theme_dsi_facet() who_countries |>   count(who_region, is_pic) |>   ggplot(aes(reorder(who_region, n), n, fill = is_pic)) +   geom_col() +   coord_flip() +   scale_y_dsi_col() +   facet_wrap(~ is_pic, labeller = as_labeller(     c(`TRUE` = "Pacific Island Countries", `FALSE` = "Other Member States")   )) +   scale_fill_manual(values = c(`TRUE` = "#0093D5", `FALSE` = "grey70"),                     guide = "none") +   theme_dsi_facet() +   labs(title = "WHO Member States by region and PIC status",        x = NULL, y = NULL) # Vertical bars ggplot(mtcars, aes(factor(cyl))) +   geom_bar(fill = "#0093D5") +   scale_y_dsi_col() +   theme_dsi() +   labs(title = "Cars by cylinder count", x = "Cylinders", y = NULL) dsi_flextable_defaults() df <- data.frame(   region = c("AFR", "AMR", "EUR", "WPR", "SEAR", "EMR"),   countries = c(47, 35, 53, 28, 10, 21) ) ggpie(df, "region", "countries", .offset = 1.2)

title: DSIR — Data Science Infrastructure for Global Health
text: geomean() — geometric mean, optional weights. Useful aggregating ratio-based health indicators composite multiplicative — e.g. UHC service-coverage tracers.
code: geomean(c(0.6, 0.8, 0.95))                      # ~0.772 geomean(c(0.6, 0.8, 0.95), w = c(2, 1, 1))      # weighted version

title: DSIR — Data Science Infrastructure for Global Health
text: Check availability downloading. GHO thousands indicators one may cover countries years need. Three lightweight helpers ask server available without transferring observations: Fetch clean. typical workflow search → fetch → clean: gho_clean() produces unified DSIR cleaned-indicator schema — 15-column shape produced sdg_clean(), GHO SDG output can combined directly bind_indicators() (see ). Output columns: source, id, indicator, location, iso3, location_name, year, value, value_num, low, high, series, dim1–dim3. Source columns missing raw response (e.g. Low / High indicators without confidence intervals) filled typed NA.
code: # Yes / no for a given indicator + filter (TRUE / FALSE / NA on failure) gho_has_data("WHOSIS_000001", area = "FRA")  # Row count the same filter would return — useful for sizing a download gho_count("WHOSIS_000001", area = wpro_cty)  # Per-country year coverage and observation counts gho_coverage("WHOSIS_000001", area = c("FRA", "DEU", "JPN")) #>   location year_min year_max n_obs #> 1 DEU          2000     2021    66 #> 2 FRA          2000     2021    66 #> 3 JPN          2000     2021    66 # Search indicators by keyword gho_indicators("mortality")  # Discover available dimensions for an indicator gho_dimensions("NCDMORT3070") gho_dimensions("NCDMORT3070", dimension = "Dim1")  # Fetch country-level data gho_data("NCDMORT3070", spatial_type = "country")  # Fetch with area and year filters gho_data(   indicator = "WHOSIS_000001",    area      = wpro_cty,   year_from = 2015 )  # Tidy the raw GHO response raw <- gho_data("NCDMORT3070", spatial_type = "country", area = wpro_cty) gho_clean(raw)

title: DSIR — Data Science Infrastructure for Global Health
text: pattern GHO: search → fetch → clean. sdg_clean() produces unified 15-column schema gho_clean(): source, id, indicator, location, iso3, location_name, year, value, value_num, low, high, series, dim1–dim3. SDG-side fields populate id (indicator code, e.g. "3.4.1"), indicator (human-readable series description), location (UN M49 numeric code), iso3 (via m49_to_iso3() — NA region / world aggregates non-Member areas), location_name, series. GHO-dim1–dim3 columns NA SDG rows. value kept character preserve non-numeric entries ("<0.1", aggregate notes); value_num numeric coercion (NA coercion fails).
code: # Browse goals, targets, indicators, and geographic areas sdg_goals() sdg_targets() sdg_indicators() sdg_areas()  # Search indicators by keyword — AND semantics, case-insensitive # substring match on the indicator description (client-side filter) sdg_indicators("mortality") sdg_indicators("mortality cancer") sdg_indicators(c("maternal", "mortality"))  # Fetch indicator data — `area` accepts ISO3 codes (converted internally) # or UN M49 numeric codes. ISO3 lets DSIR's regional vectors be passed # directly, the same way they work with the GHO client. sdg_data("3.2.1", area = "PHL", year_from = 2015, year_to = 2023) sdg_data("3.4.1", area = wpro_cty)  # M49 also works (e.g. when copy-pasting codes from sdg_areas()) sdg_data("3.2.1", area = "608", year_from = 2015, year_to = 2023)  # Tidy the SDG response raw <- sdg_data("3.2.1", area = "PHL") sdg_clean(raw)

title: DSIR — Data Science Infrastructure for Global Health
text: cleaners produce schema, can stack output bind_indicators() use source column keep track row came : Exploring series. single SDG indicator often contains several series — example different vaccines, sex strata, causes death — country / year coverage. sdg_coverage() summarises year range observation count per (location, series) can see available deciding series analyse. GHO-style has_data() / count() helpers intentionally provided SDG SDG data generally complete enough pre-flight checks add little value.
code: gho <- gho_data("NCDMORT3070", area = wpro_cty) |> gho_clean() sdg <- sdg_data("3.4.1",        area = wpro_cty) |> sdg_clean() bind_indicators(gho, sdg) # 3.b.1 (vaccine coverage) is published as four separate series sdg_coverage("3.b.1", area = c("156", "608")) #>   location series      year_min year_max n_obs #> 1 156      SH_ACS_DTP3     2000     2023    24 #> 2 156      SH_ACS_HPV      2018     2023     6 #> 3 156      SH_ACS_MCV2     2000     2023    24 #> 4 156      SH_ACS_PCV3     2017     2023     7 #> 5 608      SH_ACS_DTP3     2000     2023    24 #> 6 608      SH_ACS_HPV      2017     2023     7 #> 7 608      SH_ACS_MCV2     2000     2023    24 #> 8 608      SH_ACS_PCV3     2014     2023    10

title: DSIR: Data Science Infrastructure for Global Health in R — DSIR-package
text: small, opinionated toolkit global health data analysis. bundles publication-ready ggplot2 theme (theme_dsi(), theme_dsi_facet()) flush-axis bar-chart scales (scale_y_dsi_col(), scale_x_dsi_col()), sensible flextable defaults (dsi_flextable_defaults()), quick pie chart helper (ggpie()), regional country-code datasets (wpro_cty) region UN M49 lookups (iso3_to_region(), iso3_to_m49(), m49_to_iso3()), geometric mean helper indicator aggregation (geomean()), thin clients Global Health Observatory API (gho_indicators(), gho_data(), gho_dimensions(), gho_has_data(), gho_count(), gho_coverage()) UN Sustainable Development Goals API (sdg_goals(), sdg_targets(), sdg_indicators(), sdg_areas(), sdg_data(), sdg_coverage()), plus unified cleaning / binding pipeline (gho_clean(), sdg_clean(), bind_indicators()) puts GHO SDG output 15-column schema.

title: Geometric Mean — geomean
text: Computes geometric mean numeric vector, optional weights. Useful aggregating multiplicative quantities ratio-based health indicators (e.g. UHC service-coverage tracers, composite index geometric mean component coverage values).

title: Geometric Mean — geomean
code: geomean(x, w = NULL, na.rm = TRUE)

title: Geometric Mean — geomean
text: x numeric vector. Zeros produce result 0. Negative values produce NaN warning, since geometric mean undefined negative numbers. w Optional numeric vector weights, length x. Must non-negative. NULL (default), unweighted geometric mean returned. na.rm Logical. missing values x (w, provided) removed computation? Default TRUE.

title: Geometric Mean — geomean
text: numeric scalar. Returns NA_real_ input empty, entirely NA, na.rm = FALSE element NA. Returns NaN warning x contains negative values, weights zero.

title: Geometric Mean — geomean
text: Pass w compute weighted geometric mean, defined exp(weighted.mean(log(x), w)).

title: Geometric Mean — geomean
code: # Unweighted geomean(c(1, 4, 16))                # 4 #> [1] 4 geomean(c(0.6, 0.8, 0.95))          # ~0.772 — typical UHC tracer aggregation #> [1] 0.7697002 geomean(c(1, NA, 4))                # 2 #> [1] 2 geomean(c(1, NA, 4), na.rm = FALSE) # NA_real_ #> [1] NA geomean(c(1, 0, 4))                 # 0 #> [1] 0  # Weighted geomean(c(1, 4, 16), w = c(1, 1, 1))     # 4 (equal weights = unweighted) #> [1] 4 geomean(c(1, 4, 16), w = c(1, 2, 1))     # weighted toward 4 #> [1] 4 geomean(c(0.6, 0.8, 0.95), w = c(2, 1, 1)) #> [1] 0.7232343

title: Tidy a GHO Data Frame — gho_clean
text: Selects, renames, type-casts useful columns GHO observation table returned gho_data(), producing compact tibble unified DSIR cleaned-indicator schema — schema produced sdg_clean(), two outputs can combined directly bind_indicators().

title: Tidy a GHO Data Frame — gho_clean
text: df data frame returned gho_data().

title: Tidy a GHO Data Frame — gho_clean
text: tibble 15 columns: source (always "gho"), id, indicator, location, iso3, location_name, year, value, value_num, low, high, series (NA), dim1, dim2, dim3. Sorted location year. Empty input returns empty tibble columns types.

title: Tidy a GHO Data Frame — gho_clean
text: mapping (GHO source → unified column) : IndicatorCode → id IndicatorCode resolved GHO indicator catalog → indicator (human-readable name; cached session level first call) SpatialDim    → location; also iso3 matches Member State, otherwise iso3 = NA TimeDim       → year (integer) Value         → value (character; raw) NumericValue  → value_num (numeric) Low, High   → low, high (numeric) Dim1, Dim2, Dim3 → dim1, dim2, dim3 (character) series column always NA GHO output (SDG-concept). location_name column populated looking location (ISO3 code region code) who_countries dataset hardcoded set regional names; locations match neither (e.g. non-Member State areas) left NA. Source columns absent df (e.g. Low / High indicators without confidence intervals) filled typed NA, output always 15 columns column types. GHO data endpoint (/api/{IndicatorCode}) return IndicatorName; field lives catalog endpoint queried gho_indicators(). first call within R session, gho_clean() fetches catalog caches rest session, indicator column carries full human-readable indicator name. catalog fetched (e.g. network), gho_indicators() emits warning indicator column falls back NA.

title: Convert UN M49 Numeric Codes to ISO3 Codes — m49_to_iso3

title: Convert UN M49 Numeric Codes to ISO3 Codes — m49_to_iso3

what: Tidy an SDG Data Frame — sdg_clean
title: Tidy an SDG Data Frame — sdg_clean
text: Selects, renames, type-casts useful columns SDG observation table returned sdg_data(), producing compact tibble unified DSIR cleaned-indicator schema — schema produced gho_clean(), two outputs can combined directly bind_indicators().

title: Tidy an SDG Data Frame — sdg_clean
code: sdg_clean(df)

title: Tidy an SDG Data Frame — sdg_clean
text: df data frame returned sdg_data().

title: Tidy an SDG Data Frame — sdg_clean
text: tibble 15 columns: source (always "sdg"), id, indicator, location, iso3, location_name, year, value, value_num, low, high, series, dim1 (NA), dim2 (NA), dim3 (NA). Sorted location year. Empty input returns empty tibble columns types.

title: Tidy an SDG Data Frame — sdg_clean
text: mapping (SDG source → unified column) : indicator (list-column, flattened) → id (e.g. "3.4.1") seriesDescription                  → indicator (human-readable label; NA API response include ) geoAreaCode                        → location (UN M49 numeric, character); also iso3 via m49_to_iso3() Member States — region / world aggregates non-Member areas get iso3 = NA location_name resolved looking iso3 who_countries (Member State location_name gho_clean() output), fallback SDG API's raw geoAreaName non-Member-State rows (e.g. regional / world aggregates) timePeriodStart                    → year (integer) value                              → value (character; raw) value_num (numeric; NA non-numeric entries like "<0.1" aggregate notes) lowerBound, upperBound           → low, high (numeric) series                             → series Three columns always present never populated SDG output: dim1, dim2, dim3 (GHO-concepts).

what: Explore Series Coverage of an SDG Indicator — sdg_coverage
title: Explore Series Coverage of an SDG Indicator — sdg_coverage
text: single SDG indicator (example "3.4.1", NCD mortality) typically published several series stratified sex, age, cause. Different series may different country year coverage. sdg_coverage() summarises year range observation count per (location, series) combination, can see series exist indicator one covered committing downstream analysis.

title: Explore Series Coverage of an SDG Indicator — sdg_coverage
code: sdg_coverage(indicator, area = NULL, year_from = NULL, year_to = NULL)

title: Explore Series Coverage of an SDG Indicator — sdg_coverage
text: indicator Character vector SDG indicator codes (e.g. "3.4.1"). area Character vector country/area codes. Accepts either ISO3 codes (e.g. c("PHL", "FRA")) — converted automatically via iso3_to_m49() — UN M49 numeric codes (e.g. c("608", "250")) returned sdg_areas(). mix two formats single call. Default NULL returns areas. Unknown ISO3 codes dropped warning network call. year_from Numeric. Start year filter (inclusive). Default NULL. year_to Numeric. End year filter (inclusive). Default NULL.

title: Explore Series Coverage of an SDG Indicator — sdg_coverage
text: tibble one row per (location, series) columns: location (chr) — area code (geoAreaCode). series (chr) — SDG series code. year_min (int) — earliest year data. year_max (int) — latest year data. n_obs (int) — number observations. Sorted location series. Empty input service failure returns empty tibble five columns.

title: Explore Series Coverage of an SDG Indicator — sdg_coverage
text: Unlike GHO availability helpers, function series-exploration tool rather payload-saving precheck: SDG data generally complete enough GHO-style has_data() / count() helpers add little value, intentionally provided. SDG API also offers payload-reduction option ($select equivalent), sdg_coverage() calls sdg_data() internally aggregates result client-side.

title: Explore Series Coverage of an SDG Indicator — sdg_coverage
code: # \donttest{ # Series available for NCD mortality in China and Brazil sdg_coverage("3.4.1", area = c("156", "076")) #> Fetching: #> <https://unstats.un.org/sdgs/UNSDGAPIV5/v1/sdg/Indicator/Data?indicator=3.4.1&pageSize=1000&areaCode=156&areaCode=076&page=1> #> # A tibble: 2 × 5 #>   location series      year_min year_max n_obs #>   <chr>    <chr>          <int>    <int> <int> #> 1 156      SH_DTH_NCOM     2000     2021    21 #> 2 76       SH_DTH_NCOM     2000     2021    21  # Filter to a year range sdg_coverage("3.4.1", area = "156", year_from = 2015) #> Fetching: #> <https://unstats.un.org/sdgs/UNSDGAPIV5/v1/sdg/Indicator/Data?indicator=3.4.1&pageSize=1000&areaCode=156&page=1> #> # A tibble: 1 × 5 #>   location series      year_min year_max n_obs #>   <chr>    <chr>          <int>    <int> <int> #> 1 156      SH_DTH_NCOM     2015     2021    12 # }

title: List SDG Indicators — sdg_indicators
text: search Optional character. Search keywords matched description column (case-insensitive). terms must match (semantics). Accepts either: single string, split whitespace terms (e.g. "mortality cancer" keeps rows whose description contains "mortality" "cancer"), character vector, whose elements used terms verbatim (term may contain whitespace, e.g. c("mortality rate", "attributed")). filter applied client-side using grepl() fixed = TRUE UN SDG /Indicator/List endpoint OData exposes server-side search parameter; full list small (~250 rows) cheap.

title: DSIR ggplot2 theme — WHO publication-style — theme_dsi
text: clean, modern theme tuned global-health publications. Removes panel border, draws light grid lines, uses muted text colour data — chart chrome — visual focus. grid argument controls direction(s) grid lines run, theme works equally well vertical bars, horizontal bars (via coord_flip()), scatter plots, line charts.

title: DSIR ggplot2 theme — WHO publication-style — theme_dsi
text: base_size Base font size points. Default 12. base_family Base font family. Default "" (system default). Set "sans", "Arial", "Helvetica", "Calibri" specific look. Empty default keeps package portable CRAN's Linux check machines, Calibri unavailable. accent Accent colour used axis lines default highlight elements. Default "#0093D5" (blue). Pass colour string. grid_color Colour major grid. Default "grey92" — light grey, close bbplot OECD style. grid direction(s) draw major grid lines. One "" (default — draws horizontal vertical, works correctly coord_flip()), "y" (horizontal grid lines, look used DSIR <= 0.5.x), "x" (vertical grid lines), "none". legend_position Position legend. Default "bottom". Pass "none", "top", "right", numeric vector c(x, y).

title: DSIR ggplot2 theme — WHO publication-style — theme_dsi

title: DSIR ggplot2 theme — WHO publication-style — theme_dsi
code: library(ggplot2)  # Default — grid in both directions, works under coord_flip() ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt, mpg, color = factor(cyl))) +   geom_point(size = 3) +   theme_dsi() +   labs(title = "Fuel efficiency by weight",        x = "Weight (1000 lbs)", y = "Miles per gallon",        color = "Cylinders")   # Minimal look — only horizontal grid lines ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt, mpg)) +   geom_point(size = 3, color = "#0093D5") +   theme_dsi(grid = "y") +   labs(x = "Weight (1000 lbs)", y = "Miles per gallon")

title: DSIR ggplot2 theme for faceted plots — theme_dsi_facet
text: sibling theme_dsi() tuned faceted plots. theme_dsi() uses half-frame axis lines horizontal grid lines — look suits single panel — repeating across every facet looks heavy panels run together. theme_dsi_facet() replaces axis lines light panel border, draws grid lines axes, gives strip soft background, inserts whitespace panels.

title: DSIR ggplot2 theme for faceted plots — theme_dsi_facet
text: base_size Base font size points. Default 12. base_family Base font family. Default "" (system default). Set "sans", "Arial", "Helvetica", "Calibri" specific look. Empty default keeps package portable CRAN's Linux check machines, Calibri unavailable. accent Accent colour, kept parity theme_dsi() argument set interchangeable. currently used facet variant — panel border replaces accent-coloured axis lines. Default "#0093D5" (blue). grid_color Colour major grid (axes). Default "grey92". strip_fill Background fill colour facet strips. Default "grey95" — light neutral grey. Avoid blues, clash -blue accent. strip_color Text colour inside facet strips. Default "grey20". grid direction(s) draw major grid lines. One "" (default — horizontal vertical), "y" (horizontal), "x" (vertical), "none". Matches identical argument theme_dsi() two themes can swapped without changing settings. legend_position Position legend. Default "bottom". Pass "none", "top", "right", numeric vector c(x, y).

title: WHO Member States with regional and Pacific classifications — who_countries
text: tibble 194 rows 7 columns: iso3 ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 code (3-letter, e.g. "PHL"). iso2 ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 code (2-letter, e.g. "PH"). m49_code UN M49 numeric code, stored 3-character string leading zeros present (e.g. "008" Albania). Stored character downstream APIs (notably UN SDG API) expect leading-zero form. name_official official English name (e.g. "Iran (Islamic Republic )"). Use formal documents reports. name_short shorter form suitable charts tables (e.g. "Iran"). Equal name_official countries whose official name already concise. See Details. who_region region code: one "AFR", "AMR", "SEAR", "EUR", "EMR", "WPR". is_pic Logical. TRUE 14 Pacific Island Country (PIC) Member States WPR, FALSE otherwise.

Examples: "DPR Korea", "DR Congo", "Lao PDR", "United Kingdom". short forms follow conventions used regional reports OECD Health Glance: Asia/Pacific. PICs. title: DSIR 0.7.0
text: gho_clean() output longer 11 columns; 15. New columns include source ("gho"), iso3 (ISO3 alpha-3 code spatial dim matches Member State, NA otherwise), location_name (resolved who_countries regional codes; see bug-fix note ), series (always NA — SDG-concept). sdg_clean() output longer 10 columns reshaped unified schema. Several columns moved renamed: SDG goal target columns longer carried forward; SDG indicator code now id (indicator); human-readable series description moved indicator (present ); new iso3 column holds ISO3 alpha-3 code via m49_to_iso3() Member States. cleaners now coerce value_num / low / high numeric; non-numeric raw values (e.g. SDG "<0.1") yield NA columns value keeps raw character form. cleaners now coerce year integer.

title: DSIR 0.7.0
text: New bind_indicators(...): stacks number tibbles returned gho_clean() sdg_clean() single tibble. two cleaners now share schema, result uniform can filtered, joined, visualised without source-specific code paths; use source column distinguish GHO SDG rows. New m49_to_iso3(): maps UN M49 numeric area codes ISO3 country codes, counterpart iso3_to_m49(). Accepts zero-padded ("076") bare ("76") input. Non-Member areas — including region / world aggregates — return NA. Used internally sdg_clean() populate new iso3 column.

title: DSIR 0.7.0
text: Fixed duplicate definition sdg_coverage(): function defined R/sdg.R R/sdg_coverage.R, R’s alphabetical source-file loading meant wrong copy (fragile \r composite-key separator upfront .resolve_area() call) one actually effect. surviving implementation now uses \x1f composite-key separator resolves area argument -front, documented “unknown ISO3 codes warn dropped” message surfaces cleanly sdg_coverage() rather swallowed inside internal call sdg_data(). .gho_get() now sets 20-second per-request timeout applies exponential backoff (backoff = ~ min(2 ^ .x, 30)) .sdg_get(). Previously hung upstream request stall entire gho_data() call indefinitely. Network helpers now also fail soft malformed response bodies. .gho_get(), .sdg_get(), inline call gho_count() wrap httr2::resp_body_json() tryCatch(), truncated upstream response (premature EOF) surfaces warning returns NULL / NA_integer_ instead propagating parse error caller. Previously response halt R CMD check example run, e.g. sdg_goals(include_children = TRUE) flaky UN endpoint. gho_clean() sdg_clean() now populate location_name consistently across APIs. Member States resolved who_countries$name_short cleaners, country location_name regardless source. gho_clean() additionally resolves regional codes (AFR, AMR, SEAR, EUR, EMR, WPR) GLOBAL human-readable names. sdg_clean() falls back SDG API’s original geoAreaName non-Member-State rows (e.g. regional world aggregates), preserving information who_countries carry. gho_clean() now actually populates indicator column. GHO data endpoint (/api/{IndicatorCode}) return IndicatorName, old implementation left indicator NA every row despite documented mapping. gho_clean() now lazy-loads indicator catalog (via gho_indicators()) per R session resolves IndicatorCode fill indicator column human-readable name. output schema unchanged; catalog fetched, indicator falls back NA warning surfaces gho_indicators().

title: DSIR 0.7.0
text: network examples now use \donttest{} instead \dontrun{}, CRAN reviewers can opt run .

title: DSIR 0.7.0
text: Added httptest2 Suggests. GHO SDG helpers gain offline regression tests covering empty value=[] fix, OData pagination loop, multi-value SDG indicator / areaCode repeated-key URL form, client-side SDG year filter, graceful-failure behaviour HTTP errors. Test coverage expanded gho_clean(), sdg_clean(), bind_indicators(), m49_to_iso3(), scale_*_dsi_col(), who_countries dataset, regional ISO3 vectors.

title: DSIR 0.6.0
text: theme_dsi() now draws grid lines directions default. New grid argument controls direction — pass grid = "y" restore previous look (horizontal grid ). change fixes visual glitch horizontal bar charts (made coord_flip()) grid lines running parallel bars instead crossing . theme_dsi_facet() gains grid argument mirroring theme_dsi()’s — defaults "" (unchanged behaviour); pass "y", "x", "none" control direction(s) grid runs.

title: DSIR 0.6.0
text: New scale_y_dsi_col() scale_x_dsi_col() — drop-replacements scale_y_continuous() / scale_x_continuous() remove lower axis expansion, columns bar charts sit flush axis (publication style). New theme_dsi_facet() faceted plots — uses panel borders, light-grey strip backgrounds, bilateral grid lines. New GHO data-availability helpers, screening indicator / filter combinations pulling full result set: gho_has_data() returns TRUE / FALSE / NA whether server rows indicator filter. gho_count() returns row count filter yield, using OData $count=true endpoint (rows transferred). gho_coverage() returns tibble location, year_min, year_max, n_obs per area, using $select=SpatialDim,TimeDim keep payload small. sdg_indicators() gains search parameter, mirroring gho_indicators(search). Accepts either single string (split whitespace terms) character vector (terms used verbatim). terms must match (semantics, case-insensitive substring indicator description column). filter applied client-side UN SDG /Indicator/List endpoint OData exposes server-side search parameter; list small enough (~250 rows) cheap. New sdg_coverage(): series-exploration helper SDG indicators. SDG indicator (e.g. "3.4.1") typically contains several series stratified sex, age, cause; sdg_coverage() returns tibble (location, series, year_min, year_max, n_obs) can see series exist covered. intentionally framed series-exploration rather gho_has_data() / gho_count()-style availability precheck — SDG data generally complete, screening helpers deliberately provided SDG. New geomean(): geometric mean numeric vector, optional weights via w. Useful aggregating ratio-based health indicators UHC service-coverage tracers. Handles NA (removed default), zeros (returns 0), negative values (warns returns NaN). New iso3_to_m49(): maps ISO3 country codes UN M49 numeric codes using bundled who_countries dataset. Non-Member codes return NA. Input case-insensitive. sdg_data() sdg_coverage() now accept ISO3 codes directly area argument; M49 codes continue work unchanged. DSIR’s regional vectors (wpro_cty, afro_cty, etc.) can now passed directly SDG functions, matching existing GHO workflow. ISO3 M49 mixed within single call. New iso3_to_region(): maps ISO3 country codes region codes ("AFR", "AMR", "SEAR", "EUR", "EMR", "WPR") using bundled who_countries dataset. Pass long = TRUE full names ("Western Pacific", etc.). Non-Member codes (e.g. Associate Members like "PRI") return NA. Stays sync governance changes reflected DSIR — e.g. Indonesia WPR since EB156 (May 2025).

title: DSIR 0.6.0
text: sdg_data(): year filtering (year_from / year_to) now applied client-side. UN SDG API’s server-side timePeriodStart / timePeriodEnd parameters causing HTTP 500 errors ~30x slowdowns least indicator/area combinations (e.g. 3.2.1 area = "608"); client-side filtering avoids issue. User-facing behaviour unchanged. sdg_coverage() inherits fix automatically dispatches sdg_data(). .sdg_get() now sets 20-second per-request timeout exponential backoff retries (backoff = ~ min(2^.x, 30)), hung upstream request stall call indefinitely. internal pagination helper .gho_get() previously produced spurious 1-row, 1-column tibble (V1 type list) server returned empty result set (value = []). Empty value chunks now skipped empty tibble returned rows accumulate. affected gho_data() filters matches (e.g. indicator + area combination GHO data ) gho_indicators(search) indicator name matched search term. sdg_data() now correctly filters one indicator area code supplied. SDG API expects multi-value parameters repeated keys (indicator=&indicator=B), previous implementation joined commas (indicator=,B); API silently dropped filter returned rows. Single-value calls unaffected. sdg_data() longer errors duplicate 'row.names' allowed result spans one page. per-page row-name handling simplified.

title: DSIR 0.5.0
text: theme_dsi(): removed color argument added accent instead. semantics different: color previously controlled text colour, accent now controls axis line tick colour. used theme_dsi(color = X) 0.2.x, change theme_dsi(accent = X) note visual effect shifted “everywhere” “axis ”. Text colour now fixed grey20 follow modern publication conventions.

title: DSIR 0.5.0
text: gho_data() now correctly handles long area vectors. Previously, passing ~22 ISO3 codes hit upstream query string length limit resulted HTTP 400 errors. Switched chained clauses OData operator, generates much shorter URL supports ~115 codes per call.

title: DSIR 0.5.0
text: New gho_clean(): post-processor gho_data() output. Selects useful columns renames snake_case (id, location, year, dim1-dim3, value, value_num, low, high, indicator). Output schema stable across indicators — missing columns filled NA. See ?gho_clean details. New sdg_clean(): counterpart sdg_data(). Renames SDG API columns snake_case flattens indicator list-column. Returns value, low, high character preserve non-numeric entries ("<0.1", aggregate notes); coerce .numeric() downstream. See ?sdg_clean details.

title: DSIR 0.5.0
text: theme_dsi() redesigned follow modern publication conventions (BBC, OECD, Financial Times style): Removed panel border; axis lines left bottom Removed vertical grid lines (kept horizontal ) Lighter grid colour (grey92, grey85) Larger title subtitle Text colour standardised grey20 Title left-aligned plot edge New legend_position argument gho_data() now infers spatial_type = "country" area provided without explicit spatial_type. Pass spatial_type explicitly silence message. gho_data() now validates area argument: must non-NA, non-empty character vector.

title: DSIR 0.4.0
text: New who_countries dataset: tibble 194 Member States ISO3, ISO2, UN M49 numeric code, official short names, region, is_pic flag 14 Pacific Island Country Member States. New regional ISO3 character vectors ergonomic filtering: afro_cty, amro_cty, searo_cty, euro_cty, emro_cty, plus pic_cty. derived who_countries stay consistent master table.

title: DSIR 0.4.0
text: wpro_cty now contains 28 countries (22 0.2.0). reflects May 2025 World Health Assembly decision (EB156) reassigning Indonesia South-East Asia Region Western Pacific Region, along Cook Islands Niue, full Member States previously omitted. Code aggregates wpro_cty (sums, counts, joins) produce different results DSIR 0.2.x — intended.

title: DSIR 0.4.0
text: Added data-raw/who_countries.R single source truth country-related datasets. Editing one file regenerates master tibble derived vectors.

title: DSIR 0.3.0
text: GHO SDG network functions now return tibbles instead plain data frames, improving printing wide result tables. Tibbles inherit data.frame, existing code continues work.

title: DSIR 0.2.0
text: CRAN release: 2026-04-21 Initial CRAN submission. Renamed DSI avoid name clash existing CRAN DSI package.

title: DSIR 0.2.0
text: gho_dimensions() returns unique values dimension column given GHO indicator, make easier discover breakdowns (sex, age, region, etc.) available calling gho_data(). sdg_targets() lists Sustainable Development Goal targets UN SDG API, complementing sdg_goals() sdg_indicators().

title: DSIR 0.2.0
text: theme_dsi() dsi_flextable_defaults() now default device / package default font (base_family = ""), work systems Cambria installed. Pass base_family = "Cambria" get original look. ggpie() gains input validation uses tidy-evaluation helpers internally, R CMD check longer reports undefined global variables. gho_*() sdg_*() now fail gracefully remote service unreachable, per CRAN policy packages hit network. warn return empty data frame (NULL) instead raising hard error. Full roxygen2 help pages every exported function, cross-references related functions. runnable examples use \donttest{} (\dontrun{}) network-dependent code, CRAN reviewers can opt running .
